Rearrange the words to make meaningful sentences 
 come/ Where/ ,Tony/ do/ you/ from/?/
answers: Where come from you do, Tony?
Where do you come from, Tony?
Where you do come from, Tony?
do you come from where, Tony?

questions.question:
Rearrange the words to make meaningful sentences 
 The/ will/ sunny/ next/ weather/ be/ week/./
answers: The weather will be sunny next weekend.
The weather is sunny next weekend.
The weather was sunny next weekend.
The weather are sunny next weekend.
questions.correctAnswer: The weather will be sunny next weekend.

questions.question:
Rearrange the words to make meaningful sentences 
 The/ cheap/ in/ Ha Long/ food/ is/ Bay/./
answers: Ha Long Bay is cheap in the food.
The food in Ha Long Bay is cheap.
The food is cheap in Ha Long Bay.
The food is in Ha Long Bay cheap.
questions.correctAnswer: The food is cheap in Ha Long Bay.

questions.question: Which sentence uses the correct comparative form?
answers: This book is more interesting than that one.
This book is interestinger than that one.

questions.question:

 and/ Chocolate/ lot/ of/ fat/ has/ a/ sugar/.
answers: Chocolate has a lot and of fat sugar.
Sugar has a lot of fat and chocolate.
Fat has a lot of chocolate and sugar.
Chocolate has a lot of fat and sugar.
questions.correctAnswer: Chocolate has a lot of fat and sugar.

questions.question:

questions.correctAnswer: New York is more beautiful than London.
